title: Tee markers: which tees should you play?
microFact: Playing from tees too long for you is the most self-punishing ego move in golf.
lesson:
Each tee box has several sets of TEE MARKERS at different distances. Naming varies by club (colors like blue/white/yellow/red, or names like Championship/Members/Forward), but the principle is universal: back tees = longest, forward tees = shortest.

The unwritten rule smart golfers know: play the tees that match your driving distance, not your ego. A rough guide — if your driver carries under 200m, the forward or middle tees are your tees. "Playing the tips" (the very back) as a 110-shooter turns par 4s into unreachable slogs and wrecks pace of play, which annoys everyone behind you.

Nobody respected at a club judges tee choice — they judge slow play. Announcing "I'll play the whites" confidently is normal. You must tee up BETWEEN the markers, up to two club-lengths behind them, never in front.

visual.caption: The teeing area: between the markers, up to two club-lengths back. Never ahead.

quiz.question: Where may you legally tee your ball?
options: Between the markers, up to two club-lengths behind them
In front of the markers if you're a beginner
Level with or behind the markers, anywhere on the mown area
quiz.explanation: The teeing area is two club-lengths deep, starting at the line between the markers. In front = penalty in stroke play.
